nodes.title: Base Prompt
widgets_values: Ultra-realistic cinematic fashion and fitness portrait video, soft natural studio light, neutral indoor wall background, black athletic outfit, long loose blonde hair, stable medium framing, subtle handheld micro-sway, sharp focus, natural skin texture, smooth body mechanics, 24fps feel.

nodes.title: Negative Prompt
widgets_values:
cartoon, anime, 3d render, cgi, low quality, blurry, glitch, distortion, 
text, watermark, subtitles, deformed, ugly, static pose, stiff motion, 
jittery movement, teleporting, duplicated limbs, extra fingers

nodes.title: Load Image Initial Start and Anchor Frame

widgets_values:
Camera: Stable medium portrait framing, straight-on with a slight three quarter angle, soft natural studio light, gentle handheld micro-sway.  
Action: The blonde woman stands against a neutral indoor wall in a black athletic crop top and high-waisted leggings. Her layered silver necklace, navel piercing, loose wavy hair, and forearm tattoo remain visible. She looks directly into the camera with calm confidence, breathing naturally and shifting her weight slightly.  
---
Camera: Subtle handheld portrait shot, same indoor studio background, steady waist-up composition.  
Action: Her expression softens into a faint smile. She raises one hand near her collarbone and lightly adjusts a strand of loose blonde hair while keeping eye contact with the camera. The necklace catches the light as her shoulders relax.  
---
Camera: Gentle natural sway, straight-on portrait view with slight stabilization drift.  
Action: She begins speaking quietly to the camera, making small expressive hand gestures near her waist. Her black athletic outfit stays crisp against the simple gray wall, and her loose hair moves softly around her face.  
---
Camera: Slow three quarter side turn, then easing back toward the front, controlled studio framing.  
Action: She turns her torso slightly, showing the curve of her shoulder and the tattoo on her forearm, then looks back to the viewer. Her face brightens with a confident smile and her hair swings naturally with the turn.  
---
Camera: Stable medium shot with a small push-in feeling, soft indoor light, shallow portrait depth.  
Action: She takes a small step closer and continues speaking with relaxed energy. One hand rests briefly near her hip while the other gestures naturally, preserving the jewelry, black outfit, and loose blonde hair from the start image.  
---
Camera: Slightly more animated handheld sway while keeping the neutral studio wall and front-facing portrait composition.  
Action: She laughs softly, tilts her head, and brushes her hair back from one side of her face. Her expression becomes warmer and more playful, with direct eye contact and smooth natural body movement.  
---
Camera: Three quarter front portrait, subtle stabilization drift, natural light on her face and hair.  
Action: She shifts into a confident fashion-fitness pose, shoulders back and chin slightly lifted. Her hands move briefly to frame her waist before relaxing again, necklace and navel piercing glinting as she smiles at the camera.  
---
Camera: Clean front-facing medium portrait, gentle handheld finish, steady indoor studio framing.  
Action: She ends with a bright confident smile, holding eye contact with the viewer. Her loose blonde hair settles over her shoulders, tattoo visible at her side, black athletic outfit unchanged, calm studio portrait energy preserved.

nodes.title: Wan Looper Node Settings
widgets_values:
# WAN LOOPER SVI SETTINGS

## SAMPLING

**`steps` / `split_step`**: total denoise steps, then where the HIGH model hands off to the LOW model.

**`control after generate=randomize`** changes `initial_seed` between queued runs. **`seed_mode=fixed`** still reuses that run's `initial_seed` for every segment, which is best for identity and scene stability. **`seed_mode=randomize`** changes later segments.

## SEAMS

**`overlap`**: frames blended at each seam. **`startup_trim`** removes leading frames from segment 2+ before blending. `overlap=5` + `startup_trim=5` is the audit-tested seam baseline; this workflow keeps `trim=0` to preserve all decoded frames.

**`overlap_mode` / `overlap_side`**: KJNodes blend style and seam direction. `linear_blend` + `source` is the normal/default handoff.

## ANCHORS

**Loop Config `anchor_image` is prioritized over all anchor modes for that segment.**

**`anchor_mode=fixed_initial`**: every segment uses the original start image unless a Loop Config supplies `anchor_image`. Extracted anchors are still returned, but they do not drive later segments.

**`anchor_mode=dynamic_every_segment`**: segment 1 uses the start image. Segment 2+ uses the anchor extracted from the previous segment. This is the true rolling-anchor mode.

**`anchor_mode=keyframe_schedule`**: keep using the current scheduled anchor until a listed segment finishes, then promote that segment's extracted anchor for following segments. Example `1,5,7` promotes anchors after segments 1, 5, and 7. The keyframe schedule parameters are ignored unless `anchor_mode=keyframe_schedule`.

**`anchor_frame_offset`** counts backward from the end of the decoded segment. `-1` = final frame. `-5` = fifth frame from the end, which is safer if tail frames are unstable.

**`color_correction`** nudges extracted anchors toward the start image color stats. It matters most when extracted anchors are reused by `dynamic_every_segment` or `keyframe_schedule`; with `fixed_initial` it mainly affects the `last_extracted_anchor` output.

## STITCHING

**`stitch_mode=workflow_style`**: keep the full decoded segment for assembly. **`trim_to_anchor`** cuts each segment after the extracted anchor frame.

nodes.title: Resolutions
widgets_values:
# Resolutions

*These notes were written by an LLM, and I encourage you to experiment with resolutions and see what works best for you*


Wan 2.2 I2V A14B officially targets two area tiers:

## Official 480P tier

- `832x480` landscape
- `480x832` portrait

## Official 720P tier

- `1280x720` landscape
- `720x1280` portrait

For I2V, Wan's official inference treats size as an area target; the output aspect ratio follows the input image. In ComfyUI workflows that expose exact `width` / `height`, use multiples of 16 and stay near the 480P or 720P area tiers.

## 3:4 / Portrait Photo

Wan2.2 I2V does not list official 3:4 presets, but this is a common portrait-photo aspect ratio. For I2V, preserving the start image aspect ratio is usually preferable to forcing the official 9:16 portrait preset.

Good ComfyUI 3:4 examples:

- `480x640` conservative 3:4 portrait size, lighter than the official 480P area tier
- `528x704` balanced 3:4 portrait size, closer to the official 480P area tier
- `720x960` 3:4 portrait size at a higher area, heavier than the official 480P tier but below square 720P area


## 1:1 / Square

Wan2.2 I2V does not list official square presets, but official I2V sizing preserves the input image aspect ratio while using the selected 480P or 720P area tier.

Good ComfyUI square examples:

- `624x624` near the official 480P area tier
- `640x640` common practical square size, slightly above official 480P area
- `960x960` square equivalent of the official 720P area tier

nodes.title: Base Prompt
widgets_values:
# Base Prompt

> **Note:** The base prompt is only used if no custom prompting is supplied in the loop config nodes.

nodes.title: Custom Anchor Frames
widgets_values:
# Custom Anchor Frames

> **Note:** Hook up either of the two anchor frames below to any loop config node to override the anchor frame for that segment.

nodes.title: Bookmarks
widgets_values:
# Bookmarks

> **Note:** Use 0,1,2,3,4 to jump to various sections of the workflow

# rgthree-comfy Dependency

This advanced workflow uses **Bookmark (rgthree)** nodes for quick navigation between major workflow sections.

Repository:

[rgthree/rgthree-comfy](https://github.com/rgthree/rgthree-comfy)

Install `rgthree-comfy` into your ComfyUI `custom_nodes` folder, then restart ComfyUI before loading this workflow.

The bookmarks are UI/navigation helpers only. They do not affect generation output. If `rgthree-comfy` is missing, ComfyUI may show missing bookmark nodes, but the rest of the workflow can still run once any missing UI-only nodes are ignored or removed.

nodes.title: LoRA Injection
widgets_values:
# LoRA Injection

> **Note:** Add additional LoRAs to a loop. The default method use here is connecting the global lora stack so the individual loop LoRA loaders add to the stack.

The Loop LoRA nodes can be connected to one or more loop config nodes and/or chained together with other loop LoRA nodes to add to the stack (mix and match, many possibilities).

nodes.title: LoRA Stack
widgets_values:
# LoRA Stack

Add your LoRA stack here or convert to rgthree Power Lora Loader.

nodes.title: Model Set Nodes
widgets_values:
# Model Set Nodes

These send models to individual loop sections for custom LoRA injection per segment.

nodes.title: Prompt Segment Selector
widgets_values:
# Prompt Segment Selector Dependency

This advanced workflow uses my custom **Prompt Segment Selector** node to manage per-segment prompt text before it is sent into the Wan Loop Config nodes.

Repository:

[BarleyFarmer/ComfyUI-PromptSegmentSelector](https://github.com/BarleyFarmer/ComfyUI-PromptSegmentSelector)

Install it into your ComfyUI `custom_nodes` folder, then restart ComfyUI before loading this workflow.

The workflow can still be rewired manually without this node by connecting regular text/prompt nodes directly into each `Wan Loop Config SVI` prompt input.

nodes.title: Frame Interpolation
widgets_values:
# RIFE VFI Dependency

This advanced workflow uses **RIFE VFI** from **ComfyUI-Frame-Interpolation** to create a smoother interpolated output after WanLooper generation.

Extension: [Fannovel16/ComfyUI-Frame-Interpolation](https://github.com/Fannovel16/ComfyUI-Frame-Interpolation)

Model used: [`rife49.pth`](https://huggingface.co/marduk191/rife/resolve/main/rife49.pth)

`RIFE VFI` is a post-processing helper, not required for the `Wan Looper SVI` node itself. You can remove or bypass the RIFE path if you only want the base WanLooper output.

If the checkpoint is not downloaded automatically, place `rife49.pth` in the frame interpolation extension’s RIFE checkpoint folder.

nodes.title: GGUF Model Loader
widgets_values: - **[ComfyUI-GGUF](https://github.com/city96/ComfyUI-GGUF)** — used by the advanced workflow's active GGUF model loader path. The bypassed FP8/KJ diffusion loaders are included as an alternate path for users who prefer full checkpoint loading.
